title: The Empress's Revenge
content:
The Emperor's protection gave Li Wei a sense of security, but she knew that it wouldn't last forever. The Empress was powerful, and she would find a way to strike back.

Sure enough, a few days later, Li Wei was summoned to the Empress's quarters.

"Lady Li," the Empress said, smiling. "I have a gift for you."

She motioned to a servant, who brought in a beautiful silk dress.

"This is for you," the Empress said. "I had it made especially for you."

Li Wei looked at the dress, her heart racing. She knew that this was a trap, but she couldn't refuse the Empress's gift.

"Thank you, Empress," Li Wei said, taking the dress.

The Empress smiled. "You're welcome. I hope you'll wear it to the festival tomorrow."

Li Wei nodded. "I will."

***

That night, Li Wei examined the dress carefully. She found a small pouch sewn into the lining, filled with a white powder.

She took it to Lady Wang.

"What is this?" Li Wei asked.

Lady Wang examined the powder. "This is poison," she said. "It's called 'Eternal Sleep.' It's slow-acting, but it's deadly."

Li Wei's heart raced. "The Empress wants to kill me."

Lady Wang nodded. "But you can use this against her."

"How?"

"At the festival tomorrow," Lady Wang said, "you need to pretend to be poisoned. Then, when the Emperor asks who did it, you tell him about the dress."

Li Wei hesitated. "But what if he doesn't believe me?"

"He will," Lady Wang said. "The Empress has been trying to get rid of you for weeks. The Emperor knows that."

Li Wei nodded. "Okay."

***

The next day, the festival was held in the palace's main courtyard. There were performances, food, and music. Li Wei wore the dress that the Empress had given her.

As she walked through the crowd, she felt a strange sensation in her chest. The poison was starting to take effect.

She stumbled, and the Emperor rushed to her side.

"Li Wei! What's wrong?"

"I... I don't feel well," Li Wei said, pretending to be weak.

The Emperor picked her up and carried her to a nearby chair.

"Get a doctor!" he shouted.

A doctor arrived and examined Li Wei.

"Your Majesty," the doctor said, "Lady Li has been poisoned."

The Emperor's face turned dark. "Poisoned? Who would do this?"

Li Wei looked at the Empress, who was standing nearby. "The Empress gave me this dress," she said. "There was poison in the lining."

The Emperor looked at the Empress, his eyes filled with anger.

"Is this true?"

The Empress's face turned pale. "No! I didn't do it!"

But the Emperor didn't believe her. He ordered his guards to search the Empress's quarters, and they found more poison.

"You're under arrest," the Emperor said, his voice cold.

The Empress was taken away, screaming.

***

Later that night, Li Wei recovered from the poison. Lady Wang had given her an antidote before the festival.

"You did it," Lady Wang said, smiling.

Li Wei nodded, but she felt guilty. She had framed the Empress, even though the Empress had been trying to kill her.

"Is this the right thing to do?" Li Wei asked.

Lady Wang looked at her, her eyes serious. "In the palace, Li Wei, you have to do whatever it takes to survive. The Empress would have done the same to you."

Li Wei nodded. "I know."

But she couldn't help but feel that she had crossed a line.

The next day, the Emperor came to see Li Wei.

"Li Wei," he said, taking her hand. "I'm sorry about what happened."

"It's okay," Li Wei said.

The Emperor smiled. "You're brave, Li Wei. And I'm proud of you."

He kissed her, and Li Wei felt a sense of relief. She had survived another attack, and the Empress was out of the way.

But she knew that this was not the end. There were still other people in the palace who wanted to hurt her, and she would have to be prepared.

The game was far from over, and Li Wei was determined to win.
